require "std"

def solve(nums : Array(Int64), edges : Array(Array(Int64))) : Int64
    n = nums.len
    
    # 1. Build Adjacency Matrix
    adj = Array(n) { Array(n) { false } }
    edges.each do |edge|
        u = edge[0].to_int
        v = edge[1].to_int
        adj[u][v] = true
        adj[v][u] = true
    end

    count = 0_int64
    
    # Iterate through all 2^N - 1 non-empty subsets represented by masks
    max_mask = 1_int64 << n
    
    (1...max_mask).each do |mask_int64|
        mask = mask_int64
        
        # --- Check Parity and Calculate Size ---
        sum_parity = 0_int64
        size_s = 0_int64
        
        n.times do |i|
            if (mask & (1_int64 << i)) != 0
                size_s += 1_int64
                sum_parity = (sum_parity + nums[i]) % 2
            end
        end
        
        # Condition 2: Sum must be even
        if sum_parity != 0_int64
            next
        end

        # --- Check Connectivity using BFS ---
        
        # 1. Find a starting node r (guaranteed to exist since mask >= 1)
        r = -1_int
        n.times do |i|
            if (mask & (1_int64 << i)) != 0
                r = i
                break
            end
        end
        
        # BFS Setup
        queue = Array(r)
        visited = Array(n) { false }
        
        visited[r] = true
        visited_count = 1_int64
        
        q_head = 0
        while q_head < queue.len
            u = queue[q_head]
            q_head += 1
            
            # Check neighbors v
            n.times do |v|
                # Check 1: Edge exists (u, v)
                if adj[u][v]
                    # Check 2: v is in S (i.e., in mask)
                    v_in_s = (mask & (1_int64 << v)) != 0
                    
                    # Check 3: v is not visited
                    v_not_visited = !visited[v]
                    
                    if v_in_s and v_not_visited
                        visited[v] = true
                        visited_count += 1_int64
                        queue.push(v)
                    end
                end
            end
        end
        
        # Condition 1: Induced subgraph must be connected
        if visited_count == size_s
            count += 1_int64
        end
    end
    
    count
end

def solve(nums : Array(Int64), edges : Array(Array(Int64))) : Int64
  n = nums.len
  
  # 1. Build Adjacency Matrix
  adj = Array(n) { Array(n) { false } }
  for edge in edges
    u = edge[0].to_int
    v = edge[1].to_int
    adj[u][v] = true
    adj[v][u] = true
  end

  total_count = 0

  # Iterate over all non-empty subsets (masks 1 to 2^N - 1)
  max_mask = 1 << n
  
  for mask in 1...max_mask
    
    # 2. Check Sum Parity and calculate subset size
    current_sum = 0.0
    subset_size = 0
    
    # Calculate sum and size
    i = 0
    while i < n
      if (mask >> i) & 1 == 1
        # Since nums[i] is 0 or 1, this accurately sums the values
        current_sum += nums[i]
        subset_size += 1
      end
      i += 1
    end
    
    # Check parity of the sum
    if (current_sum % 2) != 0
      next
    end
    
    # 3. Check Connectivity
    
    # Find the starting node (any node in the subset, e.g., the first one found)

      total_count += 1
    end
  end
  
  total_count
